$comment: CROSS-PLATFORM CONTRACT — adj-pgwa4. The DM conversation id MUST be derived identically on every platform (backend conversation-store.ts, iOS ChatViewModel.swift; the web uses server-provided ids and does NOT derive). Any divergence strands messages from the view (the adj-pgwa4 incident: iOS used a SPACE separator vs the backend's NUL byte). These vectors pin the derivation byte-for-byte. The first four are anchored to real live conversation ids.
spec: dm_<first 24 hex chars of SHA1( sortedPair[0] + NUL + sortedPair[1] )>
separator: U+0000 NUL byte — NOT a space. The NUL renders as whitespace in most editors; do not 'clean it up'.
sort: lexicographic on the raw UTF-8 member id strings, then join with the NUL byte

negativeGuard.$comment: If the separator were a SPACE (the adj-pgwa4 bug) instead of NUL, user+raynor would derive this WRONG id. The contract test asserts the real derivation does NOT equal it.
